raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "assertive philosophical claim emphasizing self-determination and personal agency",
  "implicit_claims": ["individuals possess significant control over their own identity and development", "external factors do not wholly determine who a person becomes", "self-creation is possible through choices and actions"],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent as metaphor but logically circular since self-making presupposes an existing self",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "context of motivation or ethics where highlighting agency encourages responsibility without denying all external influences",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "when interpreted literally to imply complete autonomy or to dismiss structural, genetic, or environmental constraints"
}
